clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [DISCLOSING_PARTY_NAME] and [RECEIVING_PARTY_NAME] (each a "Party" and together the "Parties") in connection with [PURPOSE_OF_DISCLOSURE].

This Agreement applies as a [DISCLOSURE_DIRECTION] confidentiality arrangement. Where [DISCLOSURE_DIRECTION] is "One-Way," [DISCLOSING_PARTY_NAME] is the sole discloser and [RECEIVING_PARTY_NAME] is the sole recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement. Where [DISCLOSURE_DIRECTION] is "Mutual," each Party may act as a discloser and as a recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement, and every obligation of a "Receiving Party" in this Agreement shall apply to each Party when it receives Confidential Information from the other.

"Confidential Information" means any information disclosed by a disclosing Party to a receiving Party in connection with [PURPOSE_OF_DISCLOSURE], whether disclosed orally, in writing, or in any other form, that is designated as confidential at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given its nature and the circumstances of disclosure.

Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving Party; (b) was already known to the receiving Party before disclosure, without an existing duty of confidentiality; (c) is independently developed by the receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information; or (d) is lawfully received from a third party without a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing Party.

The receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the disclosing Party's prior written consent, and shall protect Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.
The receiving Party may use Confidential Information solely for [PURPOSE_OF_DISCLOSURE] and for no other purpose.

The receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its employees, officers, or professional advisors who need to know it for [PURPOSE_OF_DISCLOSURE], provided that each such person is bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those in this Agreement before receiving the Confidential Information.

A receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by a court order, regulator, or applicable law, provided that, where legally permitted, it gives the disclosing Party prompt written notice before disclosure so the disclosing Party may seek protective measures.

Nothing in this Agreement grants the receiving Party any right, title, or license in or to the disclosing Party's Confidential Information, other than the limited right to use it for [PURPOSE_OF_DISCLOSURE].
This Agreement shall remain in effect for [AGREEMENT_TERM_YEARS] years from [EFFECTIVE_DATE], unless earlier terminated by written agreement of the Parties.

The obligations in the Duty of Confidentiality Clause and Permitted Use Clause shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of [CONFIDENTIALITY_SURVIVAL_PERIOD_YEARS] years, except that, where the Confidential Information constitutes a trade secret, the obligations shall survive for as long as that information continues to qualify as a trade secret.

Upon the disclosing Party's written request, or upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, the receiving Party shall, at the disclosing Party's election, return or destroy all Confidential Information in its possession, and shall certify such return or destruction in writing if requested, except for archival copies retained solely to comply with applicable record-keeping obligations.

The receiving Party shall not use Confidential Information to bypass the disclosing Party in order to deal directly with any customer, supplier, or business contact introduced through the Confidential Information, for a period of [NON_CIRCUMVENTION_PERIOD_MONTHS] months from the date of introduction.

Neither Party shall solicit for employment any employee of the other Party who was involved in the disclosure or receipt of Confidential Information under this Agreement, for a period of [NON_SOLICITATION_PERIOD_MONTHS] months from [EFFECTIVE_DATE], except where that employee responds to a general public job advertisement not specifically targeted at them.

If a Party materially breaches the Duty of Confidentiality Clause, the non-breaching Party may, in addition to any other remedy available, claim liquidated damages of [LIQUIDATED_DAMAGES_AMOUNT] as a reasonable pre-estimate of the harm caused by such breach, without prejudice to its right to seek injunctive relief under the Remedies Clause.

Each Party acknowledges that a breach of the Duty of Confidentiality Clause may cause harm for which monetary damages alone would be an inadequate remedy, and that the non-breaching Party may seek injunctive relief, in addition to any other remedy available at law, without being required to post a bond unless a court orders otherwise.

Where Confidential Information includes personal data of identifiable individuals, the receiving Party shall handle such personal data in accordance with applicable data protection law and shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect it against unauthorized access, loss, or disclosure.

This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute arising from this Agreement shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before either Party initiates litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.
The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE], through the signatures of their duly authorized representatives below.
